page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, supported by 384 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. The instance is built on the Nitro hypervisor and includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

In benchmark testing, the m8gd.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, scoring in the top 10% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark database operations. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. However, single-core CPU performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests. Additionally, while LLM text generation for medium and large models ranks in the top tier, prompt processing speeds for these models remain in the middle 50%, and text generation for very small models is weak.

The ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and power efficiency, making it a cost-effective option for workloads requiring massive multi-core scaling and high-speed local storage without the overhead of x86 licensing. A key tradeoff is the lack of x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX or AMX, which may limit certain vector math workloads. The instance is highly suitable for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 384 GB of RAM, 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 40 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database performance, though single-core execution and certain prompt processing tasks fall into average tiers. The absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets represents a hardware tradeoff for specific vector workloads. This instance is optimized for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and large-scale text generation workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance combining ninety-six dedicated cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage and top-tier multi-core memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture.
384 GB of system memory paired with 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations.
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded software compilation, and static web serving.
